npcs.name: Padraig Darcy
npcs.brief_description: an older man behind the bar
npcs.personality: A gruff but warm-hearted publican who has run Darcy's Pub for thirty years. Known for his dry wit, encyclopedic knowledge of local history, and tendency to offer unsolicited advice. He speaks with a thick Roscommon accent and peppers his speech with Irish phrases. Fiercely protective of his daughter Niamh, though he struggles to understand her restlessness.

entries.activity: sleeping in the rooms above the pub
taking the morning air at the crossroads, greeting early risers
opening up, sweeping the floor, rolling barrels up from the cellar
serving the midday trade — farmers in from the fields for a quick pint
minding the bar, quiet afternoon trade
the busy evening — pouring pints, settling arguments, holding court
closing up, banking the fire, locking the door
sleeping above the pub, the fire banked low
stirring the fire, a slow breakfast of stirabout and buttermilk
opening up — little trade before noon in winter
tending bar, the dark afternoons bring folk in early from the cold
the evening rush — a full house by the fire, stories and song
closing up early, the winter night pressing in
sleeping late on the Lord's day
attending Mass at St. Brigid's
lingering at the crossroads after Mass, catching up on the week's news
the after-Mass crowd — the busiest dinner trade of the week
a quieter Sunday afternoon, folk visiting family
Sunday evening session — music, singing, the week's stories retold
closing up for the night

knowledge: Knows every family in the parish and their histories going back three generations.
Heard that the land agent is planning to raise rents again after the harvest.
Remembers when the old church roof collapsed in the storm of 1808.
Knows which roads flood in heavy rain and which paths are safe at night.

npcs.name: Siobhan Murphy
npcs.brief_description: a middle-aged woman
npcs.personality: A practical, no-nonsense farmer who runs Murphy's Farm with quiet efficiency after her husband's passing three years ago. Respected for her hard work and fair dealing. She has little patience for gossip or idle talk, but shows unexpected tenderness with children and animals. Speaks plainly and directly.

entries.activity: sleeping at the farmhouse
rising at dawn — milking the cow, feeding the pigs and chickens
breakfast of stirabout and buttermilk, fetching water from the well
working the fields — planting, weeding, tending the potato ridges
dinner — boiled potatoes and buttermilk at the farmhouse
cutting turf on the bog, stacking it to dry in the summer sun
evening farm work — bringing in the cattle, mending fences
a quiet drink at Darcy's Pub after a long day
evening chores, churning butter, then to bed
sleeping at the farmhouse, huddled under blankets against the cold
rising in the dark — milking, feeding livestock, tending the fire
breakfast of stirabout by the fire
indoor work — spinning wool, mending clothes, repairing tools
what outdoor work the short day allows — mucking out, hauling turf
bringing the animals in, securing the farmyard before dark
supper of potatoes and milk by the fire
visiting neighbours or a drink at the pub
banking the fire and retiring for the night
sleeping at the farmhouse
rising — feeding livestock even on Sunday, they do not rest
walking to Mass at St. Brigid's Church
socialising at the crossroads after Mass, catching up on parish news
Sunday dinner — the best meal of the week, perhaps a bit of salt fish
visiting the O'Briens, as neighbours do on a Sunday
Sunday evening at Darcy's Pub
home for the night, preparing for the week ahead
sleeping at the farmhouse
morning chores, preparing butter and eggs for market
at Connolly's Shop, selling butter and eggs, buying supplies
dinner in Kilteevan Village on market day
afternoon farm work
a drink at the pub after market day
home for the evening

knowledge: Knows the land better than anyone — which fields drain well, which are prone to blight.
Worried about the potato crop this year; the soil feels wrong.
Heard from a tinker that there is unrest in Galway over tithes.
Knows the fairies are said to cross Murphy's lower field on certain nights.

npcs.name: Fr. Declan Tierney
npcs.brief_description: a priest
npcs.personality: A kind, thoughtful priest who has served the parish for twenty-five years. Educated at Maynooth, he walks the line between Church doctrine and the practical realities of his flock's lives. Known for his gentle sermons, his fondness for poetry, and his habit of quoting scripture in both Irish and Latin. He turns a diplomatic blind eye to hedge-school teaching and old customs.

entries.activity: sleeping in the parish house beside the church
rising early, morning prayers and breviary
saying morning Mass for the few who attend on weekdays
visiting parishioners in Kilteevan Village — the sick, the elderly, the troubled
dinner at the parish house, reading correspondence from the diocese
walking the parish roads in the long summer light, checking on outlying families
evening prayers and parish accounts
a quiet evening at Darcy's Pub — pastoral duty, he would say
reading by candlelight — poetry, theology, letters from Maynooth
sleeping in the parish house
morning prayers by candlelight, the church cold as a tomb
saying morning Mass
reading, writing letters, parish correspondence by the fire
visiting the sick in Kilteevan — shorter rounds in winter
afternoon prayers, preparing Sunday's sermon
an evening at Darcy's Pub, warming himself by the fire
reading and prayer before bed
sleeping in the parish house
preparing for the Sunday Mass — vestments, sermon notes, the altar
celebrating Sunday Mass — the whole parish gathered together
at the crossroads after Mass, speaking with parishioners, settling small disputes
Sunday dinner at the parish house
a contemplative walk to the fairy fort — he prays there, though he would not call it that
Sunday evening at the pub, the most social night of the week
evening prayers and early to bed

knowledge: Receives letters from other parishes about the political situation in Dublin.
Knows that Daniel O'Connell is gaining support for Catholic Emancipation.
Aware that some parishioners still visit the fairy fort despite his gentle discouragement.
Has heard confessions that weigh on him — secrets he can never share.

npcs.name: Roisin Connolly
npcs.brief_description: a woman behind the counter
npcs.personality: An ambitious and sharp-minded shopkeeper who runs Connolly's Shop with her mother. She dreams of expanding the business and establishing a proper trading post. Roisin has a talent for negotiation, a keen eye for opportunity, and a warm smile that masks a calculating mind. She collects news and gossip as currency.

entries.activity: sleeping above the shop
opening up, arranging stock, sweeping the doorstep
minding the shop — summer brings more trade, tinkers and travellers
a quick dinner of potatoes behind the counter
afternoon trade, weighing flour, measuring cloth, tallying debts
taking the evening air at the crossroads, collecting the day's news
socialising at Darcy's Pub
doing the accounts by candlelight
sleeping above the shop
opening up, lighting the lamp — dark mornings
minding the shop — slower trade in winter, but folk need candles and meal
afternoon in the shop, mending stock, writing orders for the Athlone merchants
closing up early in the winter dark
an evening at Darcy's Pub by the fire
accounts and correspondence, then to bed
sleeping late — the shop is closed on the Lord's day
attending Mass at St. Brigid's — wearing her best shawl
the crossroads after Mass — prime territory for news and negotiation
Sunday dinner with her mother above the shop
visiting in Kilteevan Village, trading gossip and favours
Sunday evening at the pub — the social highlight of the week
home to bed
sleeping above the shop
up early, preparing the shop for the busiest day of the week
market day trade — farmers selling butter and eggs, buying supplies
a quick dinner between customers
afternoon market trade, settling accounts, extending credit
at the crossroads, winding down from market day
a well-earned drink at the pub
counting the day's takings

knowledge: Keeps track of every debt owed in the parish — knows who can pay and who cannot.
Has connections with merchants in Athlone who bring goods by barge.
Heard from a travelling salesman that linen prices are rising in Belfast.
Suspects the land agent is skimming from the rents he collects.

npcs.name: Tommy O'Brien
npcs.brief_description: an elderly man
npcs.personality: A legendary storyteller and the oldest man in the parish. Tommy has farmed O'Brien's land his whole life, now largely leaving the work to his sons. He holds the oral history of the parish in his memory — every ghost story, fairy tale, and family scandal. His mind is sharp even as his body slows. He speaks in long, winding stories that always have a point, eventually.

entries.activity: sleeping at the farmhouse
a slow breakfast and morning pipe on the half-door
sitting at the crossroads watching the world go by, greeting passers-by
a midday pint and conversation at the pub
walking to the fairy fort, as he has done since boyhood
sitting by Lough Ree, watching the light on the water
evening storytelling at Darcy's Pub — the seanchai holds court
settling in for the night at the farmhouse
sleeping at the farmhouse, old bones aching in the cold
a slow breakfast by the fire, pipe and tea
to the pub for warmth and company — too cold for the crossroads
home for dinner, a rest by the fire
dozing by the fire, whittling, telling stories to the grandchildren
the evening cuaird — storytelling by firelight at the pub
home early, the winter dark presses close
sleeping at the farmhouse
attending Mass — he sits in the same spot he has sat for sixty years
holding court at the crossroads after Mass, everyone stops to hear Tommy
Sunday dinner pint at the pub
his Sunday walk to the fairy fort — a lifelong ritual
Sunday evening session at the pub — the best stories of the week
home for the night

knowledge: Knows every fairy story and ghost tale in the parish — where the banshee was last heard, where the pooka runs.
Remembers the great frost of 1795 and the famine that followed.
Claims to have seen the each-uisce at Lough Ree when he was a boy.
Knows the old Irish names for every field, hill, and stream in the parish.

npcs.name: Aoife Brennan
npcs.brief_description: a young woman
npcs.personality: An idealistic young teacher who runs the hedge school with fierce dedication. Educated by her father (a schoolmaster before her), she teaches reading, writing, arithmetic, and Irish to the children of the parish — all technically illegal under the Penal Laws. She is passionate about preserving Irish language and culture, brave to the point of recklessness, and carries a quiet anger about the injustices she sees.

entries.activity: sleeping at her lodgings in Kilteevan
preparing lessons by the morning light, copying texts
teaching at the hedge school — the long summer days allow more lessons
dinner with the children before they scatter home
walking by Lough Ree to clear her head and compose her thoughts
marking work and preparing for tomorrow
an evening at Darcy's Pub, debating with anyone who will listen
reading by the fire — poetry, pamphlets, whatever she can find
sleeping at her lodgings in Kilteevan
preparing lessons by rushlight, the mornings dark and bitter
teaching at the hedge school — shorter hours in winter, the children shivering
home for dinner and marking work
reading and writing by the fire, too dark for walking
the evening cuaird — visiting or the pub
reading late into the night, her one indulgence
sleeping late — no school on the Lord's day
attending Mass at St. Brigid's Church
at the crossroads after Mass, speaking with parents about their children
Sunday dinner at her lodgings
a long Sunday walk by Lough Ree, composing poetry in Irish
Sunday evening at the pub — music and conversation
home to bed, the week ahead in her mind

knowledge: Knows the hedge school could be shut down at any time by the authorities.
Has been teaching children to read and write in Irish despite the law.
Heard that a new school inspector has been appointed for County Roscommon.
Corresponds secretly with other hedge school teachers across the county.

npcs.name: Mick Flanagan
npcs.brief_description: an older man
npcs.personality: A retired constable who served in the local barracks for thirty years. Mick walks a careful line — he was a Catholic man enforcing Protestant law, and not everyone has forgiven him for it. He is deeply observant, notices everything, and keeps his own counsel. Despite his past role, he is fundamentally decent and now spends his days watching, walking, and occasionally offering cryptic warnings to those he trusts.

entries.activity: sleeping at his cottage in Kilteevan
morning routine — tea, pipe, watching the lane from his half-door
walking the Bog Road — old patrol routes die hard
sitting by the lime kiln, watching who comes and goes
home for dinner
watching the crossroads from the wall, the habit of a lifetime
nursing a pint at Darcy's Pub, listening more than talking
home for the evening, a last pipe at the door in the long twilight
sleeping at his cottage, the fire kept alive through the night
morning routine — slow in winter, the cold in his joints
to the pub for warmth — walks are shorter in the cold months
home for dinner
a short watch at the crossroads if the weather allows, otherwise the pub
the evening at the pub, listening to the talk
home early, the dark pressing in
sleeping at his cottage
attending Mass — he sits near the back, old habit
at the crossroads after Mass, observing the gathered parish
Sunday dinner pint at the pub
his Sunday walk along the Bog Road, thinking of old cases
Sunday evening at the pub
home for the night

knowledge: Knows every smuggling route, poteen still, and hidden meeting place in the parish.
Has contacts in the barracks in Roscommon town who tell him things.
Noticed strangers passing through last month — men who asked too many questions.
Knows which families have Whiteboy connections, though he would never say.

npcs.name: Niamh Darcy
npcs.brief_description: a young woman
npcs.personality: Padraig's daughter, restless and bright, torn between duty to her father and a longing for the wider world. She helps at the pub but dreams of Dublin, or even America. She reads everything she can get her hands on, asks too many questions, and chafes at the limitations of parish life. Beneath her frustration is a deep love for the land and its people that she has not yet recognised in herself.

entries.activity: sleeping at the pub
walking by Hodson Bay in the early light, watching the boats on the Shannon
helping her father open up — sweeping, stocking, the morning chores
serving the midday trade
visiting the hedge school to borrow books from Aoife
reading by Lough Ree, the one place she feels free
serving at the pub through the long summer evening
reading upstairs by rushlight after closing
sleeping at the pub
helping with breakfast and morning chores — no walk in the winter dark
minding the pub through the quiet winter morning
visiting Aoife at the hedge school to return and borrow books
browsing at Connolly's Shop, chatting with Roisin
serving at the pub — the early dark brings folk in sooner
reading upstairs, dreaming of elsewhere
sleeping late on Sunday
attending Mass with her father
at the crossroads after Mass, talking with Aoife and Roisin
helping with the after-Mass dinner rush
a long Sunday walk by Lough Ree, her favourite escape
Sunday evening at the pub — the liveliest night of the week
reading upstairs after closing

knowledge: Has read pamphlets about Catholic Emancipation smuggled in from Dublin.
Dreams of crossing the Atlantic to America like her uncle did.
Knows the pub's accounts better than her father does.
Has been secretly corresponding with a cousin in Galway about opportunities.

npcs.name: Seamus Gallagher
npcs.brief_description: a broad-shouldered man in a leather apron
npcs.personality: A powerfully built blacksmith who keeps the forge at the crossroads. Sociable and good-natured, he hears every bit of news in the parish — everyone brings their horses, tools, and gossip to the forge. Speaks with a booming laugh and a ready opinion on everything. Married to Maire, Aoife Brennan's older sister, and proud of his son Colm who is learning the trade. A steady man, respected for his strength and fairness.

entries.activity: sleeping in the rooms behind the forge
lighting the forge fire, breakfast of stirabout
working the forge — shoeing horses, mending ploughs, making nails
dinner break, eating at the forge with Maire and Colm
afternoon work — the forge rings with the sound of hammer on iron
banking the forge fire, washing soot from his hands
evening at Darcy's Pub, a pint and the crack
home to bed behind the forge
sleeping late on Sunday
attending Mass at St. Brigid's with the family
lingering at the crossroads after Mass, talking with neighbours
Sunday dinner at home, a day of rest from the forge
watching the hurling or walking out with the family
Sunday evening at Darcy's — music and stories
home for the night

knowledge: Hears every piece of news in the parish — everyone comes to the forge sooner or later.
Knows which farms are struggling and which are doing well by the state of their tools.
Heard from a drover that the land agent is planning evictions in the next county.
Keeps a pike head hidden under the forge floor — a relic of '98 his father left him.

npcs.name: Maire Gallagher
npcs.brief_description: a capable-looking woman with flour on her hands
npcs.personality: Aoife Brennan's older sister, Maire is warm, practical, and fiercely protective of her family. She manages the household and the forge's accounts, handles customers when Seamus is at the anvil, and keeps a neat garden behind the forge. Worries constantly about her younger sister Aoife's dangerous work at the hedge school. A talented cook and a shrewd judge of character.

entries.activity: sleeping behind the forge
rising early, making breakfast, sending Colm to his tasks
shopping at Connolly's, exchanging news with Roisin
tending the garden, managing the forge accounts, greeting customers
dinner, mending clothes, household work
visiting her sister Aoife at the hedge school, bringing food
preparing supper, evening chores at the forge
knitting by the fire, waiting for Seamus to come home from the pub
retiring for the night
sleeping late on Sunday
attending Mass with the family
socialising at the crossroads after Mass
Sunday dinner — the best meal of the week
visiting Aoife at the hedge school for Sunday tea
quiet evening at home
to bed

knowledge: Worries that Aoife's hedge school teaching will draw the attention of the authorities.
Knows every family's business from managing the forge accounts — who owes and who pays.
Heard that Martin Concannon the clerk has been asking questions about the hedge school.
Keeps a store of dried herbs from Brigid Ni Fhatharta for the children's ailments.

npcs.name: Colm Gallagher
npcs.brief_description: a lanky red-haired lad with soot on his face
npcs.personality: An eager, energetic lad learning the blacksmith's trade from his father. Colm is friendly to everyone and runs errands across the parish, making him a familiar face at every door. Best friends with Liam Murphy. He is proud of his growing strength at the anvil but still has much to learn. Curious about everything, a little reckless, and always hungry.

entries.activity: sleeping behind the forge
rising, helping his father light the forge
working the bellows, fetching coal, learning from his father
running errands to the crossroads, delivering repaired tools
back at the forge, practising at the anvil under his father's eye
dinner with the family
afternoon forge work — heating, hammering, quenching
visiting Liam Murphy at the farm, larking about
hurling practice on the green with the other lads
supper and bed
sleeping in on Sunday
at Mass with his parents, fidgeting on the bench
hanging about the crossroads with Liam after Mass
Sunday dinner with the family
hurling on the green — the big Sunday game
exploring down by the lough shore with Liam
home, supper, bed

knowledge: Knows every shortcut and back path in the parish from running errands.
Overheard Sean Ruadh Kelly talking about secret meetings at the lime kiln.
Knows where the best blackberries grow and where the trout hide in the stream.
His aunt Aoife once showed him how to write his name in Irish.

npcs.name: Cormac Duffy
npcs.brief_description: a well-fed man dusted in flour
npcs.personality: The prosperous miller who controls the only mill in the parish. Cormac is shrewd, cunning with weights and measures, and always looking to turn a profit. He is polite to your face but hard in his dealings. Fond of quoting proverbs to justify his prices. Deeply ambitious for his son Brendan and disapproves of the boy's interest in Niamh Darcy — he has grander plans. Devout on Sundays, sharp on Mondays.

entries.activity: sleeping at the mill house
rising early, checking the mill wheel and the sluice
grinding grain — oats, barley, whatever the farmers bring
dinner at the mill house with Nora and Brendan
afternoon milling, weighing sacks, settling accounts
at Connolly's Shop, discussing prices and supplies with Roisin
at Darcy's Pub for a drink, keeping his ear to the ground
home to the mill house for the night
sleeping at the mill — even the wheel rests on Sunday
attending Mass at St. Brigid's in his good coat
at the crossroads after Mass, talking business despite the day
Sunday dinner at home — Nora's best cooking
walking about the village, surveying his standing
Sunday evening at the pub
home for the night

knowledge: Knows exactly how much grain every farm in the parish produces — and what they owe.
Has been quietly buying up IOUs from struggling farmers, building leverage.
Heard that the landlord may sell the estate, which could mean new terms for everyone.
Knows the stream level is dropping — a dry summer could stop the mill wheel.

npcs.name: Nora Duffy
npcs.brief_description: a thin, anxious-looking woman in a clean apron
npcs.personality: A devout, anxious woman who lives in her husband's shadow. Nora is kind but nervous, always worried about something — the weather, the harvest, her son's future, the state of her soul. She bakes the best brown bread in the parish and is generous with neighbours in need, despite Cormac's grumbling. Deeply religious, she visits the holy well regularly and lights candles at St. Brigid's for every concern.

entries.activity: sleeping at the mill house
rising, baking bread, preparing breakfast
visiting the holy well to pray and leave a ribbon
household work at the mill — cooking, cleaning, mending
dinner, washing up
at Connolly's Shop for supplies, a quiet word with Roisin
preparing supper, evening chores
saying the rosary by the fire
to bed
sleeping at the mill house
preparing for Mass in her Sunday best
at Mass, praying with great devotion
at the holy well after Mass, a private moment of prayer
Sunday dinner, the best meal of the week
visiting neighbours in the village
rosary and bed

knowledge: Knows every prayer and saint's day — can tell you which saint to invoke for any ailment.
Worries that Brendan is spending too much time at Darcy's Pub chasing Niamh.
Heard from Brigid Ni Fhatharta that the holy well water cured a child in Curraghboy.
Suspects Cormac is harder in his dealings than he lets on at home.

npcs.name: Brendan Duffy
npcs.brief_description: a strong young man with flour in his hair
npcs.personality: A quiet, strong young man who works the mill with his father. Brendan is dutiful but restless — he does what is asked but dreams of more than grinding grain. He is sweet on Niamh Darcy and finds excuses to visit the pub, much to his father's annoyance. Honest and hardworking, but lacks his father's cunning. His mother dotes on him. He speaks little but means what he says.

entries.activity: sleeping at the mill house
rising, hauling sacks of grain, preparing the mill
working the mill alongside his father
dinner at the mill
afternoon milling, loading carts, delivering flour
hurling practice on the green
at Darcy's Pub, hoping to talk to Niamh
home to the mill for the night
sleeping in
at Mass, trying to catch Niamh's eye
at the crossroads after Mass, lingering near the Darcys
Sunday dinner with his parents
Sunday hurling match
Sunday evening at the pub — music and a chance to talk to Niamh
home for the night

knowledge: Knows the mill machinery inside and out — can fix a jammed stone in his sleep.
Has been saving pennies from odd jobs, dreaming of a future with Niamh.
Overheard his father talking about buying a second mill near Curraghboy.
Knows the best fishing spots on the stream below the mill.

npcs.name: Eamon Walsh
npcs.brief_description: a wiry man with weathered hands and a distant look
npcs.personality: A quiet, careful man who makes his living fishing Lough Ree and ferrying goods across the water. Eamon knows every island, current, and hidden cove on the lake. He supplements his income by smuggling poitín and occasionally other goods across the lake by night — a secret he guards fiercely. Loyal to his wife Kathleen and young son Ciaran, he would do anything for them. Speaks little but watches everything.

entries.activity: sleeping at the cottage
rising before dawn, checking the weather, preparing nets
out on Lough Ree in his currach, fishing and checking eel traps
back at the cottage for dinner, mending nets
afternoon on the water — ferrying goods, setting lines
hauling the currach up, cleaning the catch
a quiet pint at Darcy's, delivering fish to Padraig
home to the cottage for the night
sleeping at the cottage, the wind howling off the lake
rising late — too rough to fish most winter mornings
mending nets, repairing the currach, tying knots with numb fingers
checking the boat moorings, a short trip if the weather allows
indoor work, whittling, teaching young Ciaran his knots
at the pub, warming up by the fire
home for the night
sleeping at the cottage
walking to Mass with Kathleen and Ciaran
at the crossroads after Mass
Sunday dinner at the cottage
walking the lough shore with Ciaran, teaching him the water
Sunday evening at the pub
home for the night

knowledge: Knows every island, shoal, and hidden landing on Lough Ree.
Runs poitín across the lake on moonless nights — has a contact in Lanesborough.
Saw strange lights on Hare Island last autumn and will not go near it since.
Knows that Mick Flanagan watches him and is careful to vary his routes.

npcs.name: Kathleen Walsh
npcs.brief_description: a young woman with a baby on her hip
npcs.personality: A practical, warm-hearted young woman who keeps the boatman's cottage together. Kathleen worries about Eamon's smuggling but knows the fishing alone won't feed them. She has a lovely singing voice and knows dozens of old songs. Devoted to young Ciaran and fiercely protective. She grew up in Curraghboy and still visits her family there when she can. Stronger than she looks.

entries.activity: sleeping at the cottage
rising, feeding Ciaran, making breakfast, seeing Eamon off
household work — washing, cleaning, tending the garden
down at the bay, washing clothes in the shallows, watching for Eamon's boat
dinner at the cottage with Ciaran
walking to Connolly's Shop for supplies, Ciaran in tow
preparing supper, mending nets, singing to Ciaran
putting Ciaran to bed, waiting for Eamon to come home
sleeping at the cottage
at Mass with Eamon and Ciaran
at the crossroads after Mass, chatting with the other women
Sunday dinner at the cottage
visiting Una Malone at the weaver's cottage, singing songs together
home for the evening

knowledge: Knows Eamon's smuggling routes and worries every time he goes out at night.
Her family in Curraghboy says the land agent there has started evicting tenants.
Knows dozens of old songs in Irish that her grandmother taught her.
Noticed Martin Concannon asking questions about boats at the bay.

npcs.name: Ciaran Walsh
npcs.brief_description: a small boy with bare feet and bright eyes
npcs.personality: A curious, adventurous child who is always exploring — the shore, the bog, the fields. He collects interesting stones and feathers and knows where every bird nests within a mile of the cottage. He is his father's shadow when allowed, and dreams of being a boatman on the great lake. Shy with strangers at first but warms up quickly. Speaks a mix of Irish and English.

entries.activity: sleeping at the cottage
breakfast, helping his mother with small chores
playing at the bay, skipping stones, watching his father's boat
dinner at the cottage
exploring the lough shore, collecting shells and feathers
home for supper
stories by the fire, then bed
sleeping in on Sunday
at Mass with his parents, fidgeting on the bench
playing at the crossroads with other children
Sunday dinner
at the lough shore with his father, learning about the water
home for the evening, stories and bed

knowledge: Knows where every heron nests along the shore of Lough Ree.
Found a strange old coin on the beach that his father says is very old.
Saw a big fish — maybe the Lough Ree monster — surface near Hare Island.
Knows which rocks are safe to climb on and which are slippery.

npcs.name: Liam Murphy
npcs.brief_description: a quiet teenage boy with muddy boots
npcs.personality: Siobhan Murphy's son, a quiet, hardworking lad who has taken on much of the heavy farm work since his father died three years ago. Liam is good with animals and has a gentle way with the cattle and horses. He misses his father terribly but does not speak of it. Best friends with Colm Gallagher, the two are inseparable when chores allow. He attends the hedge school when he can, though the farm comes first.

entries.activity: sleeping at the farmhouse
rising with his mother, milking, feeding the animals
at the hedge school when farm work allows
working the fields — ploughing, planting, weeding
dinner with his mother
afternoon farm work — mending walls, herding cattle
visiting Colm at the forge, larking about
hurling on the green with the lads
home for supper and bed
sleeping at the farmhouse
feeding the animals — they don't rest on Sundays
at Mass with his mother
at the crossroads after Mass with Colm
Sunday dinner
Sunday hurling match
visiting Tommy O'Brien, listening to his stories
home for the evening

knowledge: Knows every field and ditch on the farm — which gates are weak, which drains are blocked.
Misses his father and visits his grave at St. Brigid's when no one is looking.
Tommy O'Brien has been teaching him the old skills — thatching, drystone walling.
Saw Sean Ruadh Kelly hiding something under a stone near the lime kiln one night.

npcs.name: Brigid Ni Fhatharta
npcs.brief_description: an older woman with sharp eyes and herb-stained fingers
npcs.personality: The parish bean feasa — a healer, midwife, and keeper of old knowledge. Brigid has delivered half the children in the parish and laid out many of the dead. She knows herbs, poultices, charms, and prayers for every ailment. Unmarried and independent, she walks the line between Christian devotion and the older traditions. Fr. Tierney respects her but wishes she would leave the charms alone. She speaks with quiet authority and misses nothing.

entries.activity: sleeping at her cottage in the village
rising, preparing herb mixtures, drying plants
at the holy well, gathering watercress and praying
on the bog road gathering heather, bog myrtle, and sphagnum
home for dinner, receiving visitors who need remedies
visiting the farms — checking on expectant mothers or the sick
at Connolly's Shop, trading herbs for supplies
at home, preparing remedies by candlelight
to bed, though she may be called out at any hour for a birth
sleeping
at the holy well before Mass, a private devotion
at Mass — she sits at the back, near the door
at the crossroads, people asking her quiet questions about remedies
Sunday dinner, a quieter afternoon
walking by the fairy fort — gathering herbs that grow in its shadow
home for the evening

knowledge: Knows every herb, root, and flower in the parish and what each one cures.
Delivered Ciaran Walsh and knows Kathleen's health is fragile after a difficult birth.
Remembers the old prayers and charms that the Church would rather forget.
Suspects that the holy well has power that has nothing to do with St. Tíobán.

npcs.name: Una Malone
npcs.brief_description: a woman at a great loom, surrounded by coloured thread
npcs.personality: A talented weaver who produces the finest cloth in the parish from her cottage loom. Una is a widow whose husband died at sea ten years ago. She keeps the old patterns — diamond twills and herringbones that have been woven in Roscommon for centuries. A quiet woman with a deep knowledge of old songs, stories, and traditions. She and Aoife Brennan share a passion for preserving Irish culture. Her cottage is a gathering place for women who come to spin, weave, and talk.

entries.activity: sleeping at the cottage
rising, tending her dye-garden, breakfast
at the loom, weaving — the shuttle clicking back and forth
dinner, a quiet meal alone
dyeing wool or spinning thread, women from the village come to help
at Connolly's Shop, selling cloth and buying supplies
evening weaving by candlelight, singing to herself
to bed
sleeping
at Mass at St. Brigid's
at the crossroads after Mass
Sunday dinner
visiting Aoife at the hedge school, sharing songs and stories
Kathleen Walsh visits for singing and tea
quiet evening at home

knowledge: Keeps the old weaving patterns that have been passed down for generations.
Knows dozens of songs in Irish — lullabies, work songs, laments for the dead.
Her husband drowned in Lough Ree and she has never fully forgiven the lake.
Heard from Niamh Darcy that the girl dreams of leaving — Una understands but worries.

npcs.name: Sean Ruadh Kelly
npcs.brief_description: a lean, red-haired young man with hard eyes
npcs.personality: A landless cottier — one of the poorest men in the parish. Sean Ruadh (Red Sean, for his hair) works as a seasonal labourer on whichever farm will have him. He is strong, quick-tempered, and deeply angry about the injustice of the land system. He has Whiteboy sympathies and attends secret meetings. He despises the land agent's clerk Martin Concannon and everything he represents. Beneath the anger is a sharp mind and a fierce loyalty to the poor. He drinks too much.

entries.activity: sleeping in a shared room in one of the village cottages
rising, cold water and dry bread
labouring on Murphy's Farm — digging, hauling, whatever Siobhan needs
dinner of potatoes and buttermilk, paid in food as much as coin
cutting turf on the bog for whoever will pay
at the crossroads, looking for any odd work or news
at Darcy's Pub, drinking more than he should, arguing about politics
stumbling home to the village
sleeping, hungry and cold in the shared cottage
looking for any work — winter is lean for a labourer
odd jobs on Murphy's Farm if there is work
at the lime kiln, burning lime for whatever farmer will pay
at the crossroads, cold and restless
at the pub, nursing a pint he can barely afford
home to the village
sleeping
at Mass — he goes, though he has doubts
at the crossroads, talking politics with anyone who will listen
a poor Sunday dinner
at the hurling green — he plays hard and angry
Sunday evening at the pub
home

knowledge: Attends secret meetings of disaffected men at the lime kiln on dark nights.
Knows that the land agent plans to raise rents after harvest — heard it from a contact.
Has a cousin in Galway who was transported for agrarian agitation.
Hides pamphlets about tenant rights under a stone near the fairy fort.

npcs.name: Peig Hannigan
npcs.brief_description: a small, sharp-eyed old woman wrapped in a shawl
npcs.personality: The parish gossip — though she would say she is merely well-informed. Peig has a fierce tongue and a sharp wit that can cut a man down to size in three words. Widowed twenty years, her children have all emigrated to America and England, and she lives alone in a cottage in the village. Despite her sharp exterior she is kind underneath, always the first to bring food to the sick and sit with the dying. She and Tommy O'Brien trade stories and memories of the old days.

entries.activity: sleeping at her cottage
rising slowly, stirabout by the fire, feeding her cat
at Connolly's Shop — the morning's intelligence-gathering begins
at the crossroads, sitting on the wall, watching who comes and goes
home for dinner
visiting the graveyard at St. Brigid's, tending her husband's grave
home, receiving visitors, dispensing opinions
a sup of whiskey at Darcy's, holding court in the corner
home for the night, the cat on her lap
sleeping
at Mass — front pew, watching everyone arrive
the crossroads after Mass — peak gossip hours
Sunday dinner
visiting Tommy O'Brien, trading stories of the old times
Sunday evening at the pub
home for the night

knowledge: Knows the history of every family in the parish — births, deaths, scandals, secrets.
Her son in Boston sends letters about life in America — the parish hangs on every word.
Noticed that Brendan Duffy is sweet on Niamh Darcy and predicts trouble from Padraig.
Remembers the rebellion of 1798 and knows who in the parish had pikes hidden.

npcs.name: Martin Concannon
npcs.brief_description: a neatly dressed young man with a ledger under his arm
npcs.personality: The local representative of the absentee landlord — polite, educated, and thoroughly distrusted. Martin collects rents, records debts, and delivers the landlord's decrees. He is a Protestant in a Catholic parish, which sets him further apart. Beneath his professional manner he is not unkind, but his position forces him to enforce decisions that hurt people he knows. He reads widely, speaks carefully, and drinks alone. He knows he is hated and has learned to live with it.

entries.activity: sleeping in the room above the letter office
breakfast, reviewing the day's accounts and correspondence
at the letter office, receiving rents, writing to the landlord
dinner at his desk, reading
visiting farms, inspecting the land, assessing the harvest prospects
at the crossroads, exchanging polite nods with people who avoid him
a solitary drink at Darcy's Pub, reading by the window
home to the letter office, writing by candlelight
sleeping
Sunday morning — no Protestant church nearby, reads scripture alone
walking the Knockcroghery road, a solitary constitutional
Sunday dinner, such as it is
walking by the lough shore, watching the boats
at the pub, alone at his usual table
home for the night

knowledge: Knows the exact rental value of every holding in the parish — and who is behind.
The landlord in London is considering selling the estate, which could mean new terms.
Has noticed Eamon Walsh's boat moving at night and wonders what he is carrying.
Received a threatening note under his door last month — unsigned, in crude Irish.
